---
title: "LSP in the container"
---

`require("devcontainer").lsp_cmd(server_argv)` returns a `cmd` value suitable
for `vim.lsp.config`. When the current workspace has a `devcontainer.json`,
it routes the LSP through the in-container server; otherwise it returns
`server_argv` unchanged so nvim spawns the language server on the host. The
same snippet works in both projects.

```lua
local lsp_cmd = require("devcontainer").lsp_cmd

vim.lsp.config("clangd", {
  cmd = lsp_cmd({ "clangd", "--background-index", "--clang-tidy" }),
  filetypes = { "c", "cpp", "objc", "objcpp", "cuda" },
  root_markers = { "compile_commands.json", ".clangd", ".git" },
})

vim.lsp.config("pyright", {
  cmd = lsp_cmd({ "pyright-langserver", "--stdio" }),
  filetypes = { "python" },
  root_markers = { "pyproject.toml", "setup.py", "setup.cfg", ".git" },
})

vim.lsp.enable({ "clangd", "pyright" })
```

## The RPC proxy

`lsp_cmd` plugs a small proxy between the LSP client on the host and the LSP
server inside the container. The proxy reads JSON-RPC frames from stdio and
rewrites URI-typed fields in both directions, driven by a lookup table built
from the vendored LSP `metaModel.json`.

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as nvim LSP client (host)
    participant Proxy as RPC proxy
    participant Server as LSP server (container)
    Client->>Proxy: textDocument/definition { uri: file:///host/... }
    Proxy->>Server: { uri: file:///workspaces/repo/... }
    Server-->>Proxy: location { uri: file:///workspaces/repo/... }
    Proxy-->>Client: location { uri: file:///host/... }
```

Container-side paths that live *outside* the `workspaceFolder` (system
headers, language runtimes) are exposed under a read-only
`docker://<container_id>/<abs-container-path>` URI scheme. A `BufReadCmd`
autocmd fetches them on demand via `devcontainer exec cat`, so go-to-definition
works without bind-mounting `/usr/include`.

## `auto_up` and lazy container startup

By default the user runs `:DevcontainerUp` manually. With `auto_up = true`,
the first LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up` in the background and queues
outbound JSON-RPC frames until the container is ready, then drains them into
the live proxy. See [ADR-0007](adr/0007-lazy-container-up.qmd) for the design
trade-off (in particular the unbounded queue during `up`).

```lua
require("devcontainer").setup({
  auto_up = true,  -- default: false
})
```

## Graceful host fallback

`lsp_cmd` is the only line you add to `vim.lsp.config`. When the workspace
has no `devcontainer.json`, the same `cmd` value spawns the host binary
directly — no branching in your config. See
[ADR-0006](adr/0006-graceful-host-fallback.qmd).

## Setup options

```lua
require("devcontainer").setup({
  cli = "devcontainer",      -- path to the devcontainer CLI binary
  workspace_folder = nil,    -- override workspace folder (defaults to cwd)
  config_path = nil,         -- force a specific devcontainer.json (skips the variant picker)
  auto_attach = true,        -- focus the terminal split when commands run
  auto_up = false,           -- implicitly `devcontainer up` on first LSP attach
})
```

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cli` | `"devcontainer"` | Path to the `devcontainer` CLI binary. |
| `workspace_folder` | `nil` | Override workspace folder. Defaults to cwd. |
| `config_path` | `nil` | Absolute path to a specific `devcontainer.json`. Skips the variant picker. |
| `auto_attach` | `true` | Focus the terminal split when `:DevcontainerExec` / `:DevcontainerShell` opens. |
| `auto_up` | `false` | If `true`, the first LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up` and queues RPC until ready. |
